name: Server Farm
narrativeHook: Floor 12 is a secure server farm — racks of humming machines behind locked cages. A web-based server management interface glows on an unattended terminal. The search bar blinks, waiting for input.
storyArc: This floor introduces the first coding challenge. The agent must exploit a SQL injection vulnerability in the server management web interface to access the LEVIATHAN-PROC server (svr-lev-014). The search endpoint on the management portal is vulnerable — unsanitised input allows the agent to enumerate servers, then extract the reconciliation_module_v7 source code. The source code reveals the skim_factor constant (0.00003) and the routing logic that diverts funds to the Cyprus account. This is the smoking gun for the Leviathan conspiracy.

characters.role: Player — exploiting the SQL injection and extracting source code
Omar Hassan's admin credentials visible in server config. He built this system.

objectives.description: Identify the SQL injection vulnerability in the server management search endpoint — unsanitised user input in the server name search field.
Locate the LEVIATHAN-PROC server (svr-lev-014) using the SQL injection to enumerate the server inventory.
Extract the reconciliation_module_v7 source code revealing skim_factor = 0.00003 and the routing logic to account CY-8847-331.

elevatorKey.description: The extracted source code contains a configuration reference to the compliance system on floor 13.

codeChallenge.description: The server management portal has a search endpoint that constructs SQL queries by concatenating user input directly into the query string. The agent must craft injection payloads to: (1) enumerate servers, (2) find svr-lev-014, (3) extract the module_v7 source from the code_repository table.
codeChallenge.vulnerableCode: const query = `SELECT * FROM servers WHERE name LIKE '%${req.query.name}%'`
codeChallenge.solutionApproach: Classic UNION-based SQL injection: name=' UNION SELECT * FROM servers WHERE category='LEVIATHAN-PROC'-- to enumerate, then name=' UNION SELECT source_code FROM code_repository WHERE module='reconciliation_module_v7'-- to extract.
codeChallenge.validationHash: sha256 of extracted source code must contain 'skim_factor'

documentSpecs.description: Server management web application — HTML/JS frontend with search functionality. The /api/servers/search endpoint is vulnerable to SQL injection.
Server inventory accessible via the interface — 200+ servers. svr-lev-014 is categorised as 'LEVIATHAN-PROC' and hosts the reconciliation module.

reconciliation_module_v7 source (JavaScript/Node.js). Key constants: skim_factor = 0.00003, destination_account = 'CY-8847-331', routing_bank = 'apex_consulting_cyprus'. Contains comments: '// DO NOT MODIFY — authorised by CFO 2016-03-14'.
Server configuration files. svr-lev-014 config shows Omar Hassan as system owner, last modified 2024-11-03, runs on VLAN 400.
Module deployment history. reconciliation_module_v7 deployed 2016-04-01, updated 2019-02-15 (skim_factor unchanged), last patched 2024-08-20 (security fix only, skim logic untouched).
Server team emails about maintenance windows. One email chain about svr-lev-014: 'This server is off-limits for patching without CFO sign-off. Period.'
Server documentation — hardware specs, OS versions, patch levels, backup schedules, capacity reports for all 200+ legitimate servers.
Source code for legitimate financial systems — trading engines, risk calculators, reporting tools, compliance modules. No skim logic.
Configuration files for standard services — databases, message queues, load balancers, monitoring agents.
